subject: Deploy dmaap-bc with downloaded certs
commit_message:
Deploy dmaap-bc with downloaded certs

This fixes the charts to properly install new SSL artifacts from AAF.
And we use the newest container version that uses cadi library to read
these artifacts.

Issue-ID: DMAAP-1401
Signed-off-by: Dominic Lunanuova <dgl@research.att.com>
Change-Id: I4d70716220a7f3e629762d3bef2149e3b0147133

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

onap-dmaap-bc is failing

Patch Set 1:

starting to look into this.  found the repo for xtesting, but searching for info on access to gitlab repo so I can get the container to reproduce the problem.  If there is a shortcut to this knowledge, please let me know.

Patch Set 1:

Finally realized that I could drill down into the healthcheck dashboard here:Full results can be seen here: https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//103625-1/index.html

I find that dmaap-bc and dmaap-dr-prov both report failure for the same reason: one of the init containers responsible for downloading the certificate reports "FAILED to get Certificate"
see: https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//103625-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-dmaap-bc-6cb9965865-fp28z-dmaap-bc-aaf-config-logs.html and https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//103625-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-dmaap-dr-prov-68cdc9d48-bjlrj-dmaap-dr-prov-dr-prov-aaf-config-logs.html

I think we are all using the same AAF versions so I am confused. Will look into the AAF setup a bit more...

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

I am able to reproduce this in my WindRiver tenant. Seems to be a problem with the keystore password.  Still investigating...
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.

Patch Set 2:

I left the deprecated properties in the configMap on purpose to be informative about change.  I don't agree that it is clutter.  Rather, they help a user more quickly determine what might be different in their config. So I'd like to keep the comments for 1 Release, and then add a Jira to remove them in next Release.  OK?
